notes: Pure delay surface. No dex; the bag is positioned on a set and recaught on the opposite toe. Foundational primitive; symbolic notation reduces to set + delay only.
Body kick into clipper position. Pure delay surface, structurally parallel to toe stall with the clipper foot as the receiving surface.
Full leg circle around the bag, recaught on opposite toe. Single sustained leg-pass that traces a complete arc around the rising bag.
NOT in freestyle_tricks. Observational entry only. Community usage suggests a same-side variant of ATW where the leg circle stays on one side of the body (vs ATW's cross-body circle).
Leg passes over the bag once, recaught on opposite toe. Distinguished from ATW by not completing a full circle around the bag.
Bag caught from below via under-foot scoop motion. The set lands low; the trick foot reaches under and lifts the bag to the recovery delay.
Toe-set dex'd outside the supporting leg, recaught on the opposite toe (in-to-out dex). James's canonical symbolic example — the template for all hippy-dex primitives.
Toe-set dex'd from outside back across to the inside, recaught on the opposite toe (out-to-in dex). Mirror of mirage: same hippy-dex mechanic, opposite directional travel.

Wing-motion dex with cross-body clipper recovery. The set lands outside; the dex flick sustains the outside arc; the recovery crosses the body to the opposite clipper. Anchor row for the butterfly-walking equivalence family.

Inside-to-outside delay combination on the hippy-dex axis. Mechanically distinct from mirage's single-dex pattern: osis carries an extended dex arc that landings on the same trajectory side as the starting hippy motion.
Rotational body-spin dex; anchor of the whirl family. The body executes a front-direction rotation during the dex moment, the kicking foot tracing through the rotation's arc.
Reverse-direction rotational dex; anchor of the swirl family. Mirror of whirl: same rotational mechanic, opposite directional travel (back vs front spin).
